own_review.issue: Argument counting via dot.getNextSibling().getChildCount(TokenTypes.EXPR) undercounts when a lambda is passed as an argument: verified empirically against checkstyle 8.8 AST that lambda arguments appear directly under ELIST as LAMBDA nodes, NOT wrapped in EXPR (e.g. forbidden(x -> x) counts 0 args, forbidden("a", x -> x) counts 1). So argumentCount matching is wrong for any call with lambda args. Note: the obvious 'fix' of raw ELIST.getChildCount() would be worse (counts COMMA tokens); correct approach is COMMA-count+1 as in OverridableMethodInConstructorCheck.getMethodParamsCount.

Doc/behavior mismatch on constructors: metadata desc says 'This check reads method & constructor names' and isForbiddenMethod javadoc says 'Check if the method/constructor call against defined rules', but the check registers only TokenTypes.METHOD_CALL — constructor calls (LITERAL_NEW, CTOR_CALL, SUPER_CTOR_CALL) are never checked, so e.g. 'new Thread()' cannot be forbidden despite the advertised capability.

Stale IntRange javadoc: field docs say 'No lower limit when null' / 'No upper limit when null' and constructor doc says 'null is equivalent to 0/infinity', but lowerLimit/upperLimit are primitive int and can never be null.

IntRange.from declares/documents CheckstyleException for invalid ranges, but a non-numeric single value without '-' (e.g. 'badArgCount') leaks an unchecked NumberFormatException from Integer.parseInt (tests only pass because TreeWalker wraps any setter exception). Also setArgumentCount clears argumentCountRanges before the loop, leaving the check in a partially-populated state if a later token is invalid.
Javadoc typo 'vaild ranges' (should be 'valid'); also isForbiddenMethod @param doc 'ruleName of the the method' has a doubled 'the' and names the parameter wrongly.

points.point: Argument counting 'fragile AST traversal'; getNextSibling assumes ELIST is immediate sibling; replace with findFirstToken(ELIST).getChildCount()
points.note: Cited code locations are real (StaticMethodCandidateCheck:629, UselessSuperCtorCallCheck:238-241 verified), so the convention observation is genuine, but the asserted risk is bogus — in the checkstyle grammar ELIST is always the next sibling of METHOD_CALL's first child — and the proposed fix (raw getChildCount on ELIST) would itself be a bug since ELIST children include COMMA tokens (2 args -> 3 children).

points.point: getChildCount(TokenTypes.EXPR) may undercount arguments if ELIST children are not always EXPR-typed
points.note: This is the real bug: verified against checkstyle 8.8 AST that lambda args sit directly under ELIST as LAMBDA nodes (not EXPR-wrapped), so counts are wrong for lambda arguments. Hedged ('if/could') and the suggested unfiltered getChildCount() fix is wrong (counts COMMAs), but the defect identification is correct.

points.point: Method name extraction via dot.getLastChild() is correct; consider adding a clarifying comment
points.note: Explicitly concludes the code is correct; pure comment suggestion.
Verified present in IntRange javadoc in the diff.

points.point:
Orphaned trailing period '

.' in checkstyle-metadata.properties desc
points.note: Verified present, but cosmetic HTML-text nit.

missed: Doc/behavior mismatch: metadata and isForbiddenMethod javadoc advertise constructor checking but only METHOD_CALL is handled
Stale IntRange javadoc referring to null for primitive int limits
IntRange.from leaks unchecked NumberFormatException despite declaring/documenting CheckstyleException; partial state in setArgumentCount on failure
exceeds: Lambda/EXPR undercount point (real bug human did not mention)

points.point: Argument counting 'works correctly but is inconsistent' with findFirstToken(ELIST).getChildCount() convention
points.note: Citations verified real, but the central assertion 'works correctly' is false (lambda args undercounted — verified against checkstyle 8.8 AST), and the claim that all cited checks use plain getChildCount() is inexact (OverridableMethodInConstructorCheck:657-676 counts COMMAs+1). Convention point itself is real, so 0 rather than -1.

points.point: Javadoc typo '@param name ruleName of the the method'
points.note: Verified present in isForbiddenMethod javadoc; doubled 'the' and wrong parameter name in description.

points.point: testEmptyRules: add comment explaining why no violations expected (default ^$ matches only empty string)
points.note: Factually accurate but a pure test-comment suggestion.

missed: Lambda argument undercount in getChildCount(TokenTypes.EXPR) — v6 affirmatively asserted the code 'works correctly' on the very line containing the bug
Doc/behavior mismatch: metadata and isForbiddenMethod javadoc advertise constructor checking but only METHOD_CALL is handled
Stale IntRange javadoc referring to null for primitive int limits
IntRange.from leaks unchecked NumberFormatException despite declaring/documenting CheckstyleException

qualitative: This is a clean, well-tested new-check PR that the human reviewer approved without findings, so most value lies in real defects beyond the reference. v5 is the stronger review: its second point lands on the one genuine correctness bug (getChildCount(TokenTypes.EXPR) undercounts lambda arguments — confirmed empirically against the checkstyle 8.8 AST where LAMBDA is a direct ELIST child), plus a verified javadoc typo, though both candidates push a 'project convention' fix (raw ELIST.getChildCount()) that would itself miscount by including COMMA tokens. v6 is weaker: it touches the same line but affirmatively declares the code 'works correctly', converting the bug into a pure style remark, and its only credited point is a javadoc typo. Neither candidate fabricated phantom APIs or false absences this round — all cited helper-check locations (StaticMethodCandidateCheck:629, UselessSuperCtorCallCheck:238-241, OverridableMethodInConstructorCheck:657-676) verify against the base commit. Both missed the advertised-but-unimplemented constructor checking and the stale IntRange null/exception javadoc issues.
